w:r.w:t: Architecture · Schema · API · Jobs · Data Flows | Version 1.0

This High-Level Design (HLD) document describes the architectural decisions, component structure, data schemas, API contracts, background job specifications, and key data flows for the Opportunity Intelligence Platform (COA). It is the companion document to COA-SRS-001. Together they constitute the full technical specification for V1 development.

This document covers: overall system architecture, component responsibilities, MongoDB schema design (all eight collections), API endpoint inventory, background job logic, and three annotated key data flows. V2 expandability mappings are included in Section 9.
Index-first query design — every query pattern has a supporting MongoDB index.
Derived never stored — confidenceLevel and dataSourceYear are always computed at read time from raw date fields.
Event-driven critical notifications — don't-miss and cancellation notifications are dispatched immediately via event trigger, not cron.
Optimistic UI — all student mutations are reflected immediately in the UI with server-side reconciliation.
Audit everything — every state-changing operation writes an AuditLog entry with a diff object.
Additive V2 — no V1 schema or API contract changes are needed to add the documented V2 features.
React 18 + React Router v6 + React Query
Vercel (Hobby) — CDN-served static assets with automatic CI/CD from main branch.
Render free tier (single dyno, 512 MB RAM). Cold-start latency ~30 s on first request after idle.
Atlas free cluster (512 MB storage, shared vCPU). Connection via MONGODB_URI env variable.
Key-value store for pre-computed Recommended feed sections. Keyed by userId. TTL: 30 min.
Gmail App Password or SendGrid free tier. Credentials stored as env variables.
Background jobs run inside the Express process. Cron expressions configured per job. Not persistent across restarts.
JWT required. Redirects to /app/feed on complete.

JWT (7-day expiry) is stored in localStorage and sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. The Express auth middleware extracts and verifies the JWT on every protected route. The middleware attaches req.user to the request object. All admin routes additionally check req.user.role === 'admin'. Source routes check req.user.role === 'source'.

Email verification uses a short-lived JWT (24 h) embedded in the verification link. On click, the backend verifies the token, sets isEmailVerified: true, and invalidates the token (single-use enforced by a token blacklist or by deleting the token record).

All collections are stored in MongoDB Atlas. Mongoose schemas enforce field types and required constraints. ObjectId references are not populated by default — only routes that explicitly need related data use .populate().
Primary key. Auto-generated by MongoDB. Immutable.
Unique. Lowercase-normalised on write. Used for auth and notifications.
bcrypt hash (cost 12). Never returned in API responses. Excluded via Mongoose select: false.
Default: false. Feed access blocked until true.
Institution branch enum. Set at onboarding. Editable in settings. Single-institution deployment — values are hardcoded.
Self-reported study year. Used directly for eligibility filtering. Editable in settings.
DomainTag slugs. Max 5. Set at onboarding. Orphaned slugs silently ignored in queries.
student | admin | source. Default: student. Changed only by admin.
{ deadlineReminders, seasonAlerts, dontMissAlerts, cancellationAlerts, emailEnabled }. All true by default.
False until onboarding flow is completed. All /app/* routes redirect to /onboarding if false.
active | suspended. Suspended users blocked from /app/* routes.
The core record. Every feed item, every notification, and every tracking entry points here.
Free text. e.g., 'Google'. Not a FK in V1.
Application link. No link-checking in V1.
internship | job | research | fellowship | hackathon | competition | scholarship | mentorship | workshop | incubator | other
paid | unpaid | unknown. Unknown renders as 'Not specified'.
DomainTag slugs. Rule-based auto-tagged on creation. Admin can override.
Eligible years (1–6). Empty = all years. Matched against User.currentYear.
upcoming | open | closed | cancelled | unknown. Transitions: upcoming→open (cron/admin), open→closed (cron/admin), any→cancelled (admin only), unknown→any (admin only).
normal | high | dont-miss. Default: normal. Admin-set.
Default: false. Admin sets true after verification. Does not auto-revoke on edit.
Weeks before deadline for prep reminder. Null = no reminder.
fixed | recurring-annual | recurring-irregular. UI display only — no job logic.
Confirmed open date. Cron sets status→open when passed.
Application deadline. Cron sets status→closed when passed.
Expected opening month (1–12). Season cron reads this.
Previous cycle's deadline. Set by cycle reset. Displayed with year label.
Not stored. Computed at read: 'confirmed' / 'estimated' / 'approximate' / null.
Not stored. Derived from lastDeadline.getFullYear() at read time.
When admin last verified the record.
Set by staleness cron. Admin clears on re-verification. Student UI shows stale warning.
Always false in V1. Reserved for V2 link checking.
Incremented on every admin save. Starts at 1. Used for optimistic concurrency.
Represents an organisation that submits listings. Called 'Organisation' in the UI.
club | department | company | admin | scraper (scraper reserved for V2).
For admin communication. Not displayed publicly.
unverified | verified | official. Controls submission flow.
Single value. Overwritten on re-verification. Full history in AuditLog.
Default: true. False blocks new submissions. No cascade to existing listings.
Primary key. Stable once created. e.g., 'frontend', 'machine-learning'.
User-facing label. Can change without breaking references.
role | skill | sector. Used to group tags in filter menus.
Default: true. False = soft-deleted. Inactive tags not shown in UI.
Optional roadmap.sh link. Display hint only in V1.
saved | applied | ignored | missed. All reversible except missed (system-set only).
Student's private scratchpad. Editable only when status = applied.
Updated on every status change. When cron sets missed, this is the listing's actual deadline.
When user first interacted. Immutable. Enables time-to-apply analytics.
The triggering listing. Kept even if listing is later deleted.
{ title, message, actionUrl }. Snapshot at creation time. Immutable.
For deadline notifications: set to listing.deadline. TTL index auto-deletes.
Specific listings this guide prepares for.
Domain tag slugs for general-purpose guides.
Default: false. Unpublished guides invisible to students.
Immutable. Never update or delete audit records. Captures every state-changing operation.
When the event occurred. Indexed descending.
userId, sourceId, or 'system' for cron jobs.
listing | user | source | tag | guide | notification
Free text. e.g., 'status_changed', 'cycle_reset', 'tag_retired'. Not enumerated — will grow.
{ field, oldValue, newValue } for edits. { from, to } for status transitions. Null for creates.
All production query patterns must be covered by an index. The following indexes are required at deployment.
Feed: open listings sorted by deadline.
Tag-based filtering on feed and calendar.
One activity record per user-listing pair.
Dashboard counts and filtered activity list.
Cron: find all saved records for a closing listing.
Pending flags per listing in admin panel.

All endpoints use REST conventions with JSON bodies. The base path is /api. Authentication is Bearer JWT in the Authorization header. Error format: { error: string, field?: string } with standard HTTP status codes.
Create student account. Returns { message: 'Verification email sent' }. 409 if email taken.
Returns { token, user: { userId, name, role, onboardingComplete } }. 403 if unverified.
Body: { branch, currentYear, interests[], notificationPrefs }. Sets onboardingComplete: true.
Body: changed profile fields. Invalidates feed cache on interests change.
Returns { closingSoon, dontMiss, recommended } in one call. Recommended served from Redis cache.
?domain[]&type[]&stipend&location&page&limit. Paginated filtered browse. Status in [open, upcoming].
Full listing + computed confidenceLevel + dataSourceYear + source name. Closed/cancelled only if user has activity.
Lightweight projection for all upcoming/open listings with timeline data. Grouped by month on frontend.
Body: { issueType, proposedFix? }. Creates DataFlag. One per (userId, listingId) — upsert.
Body: { listingId, status: 'saved' | 'ignored' }. Creates UserActivity. Unique constraint prevents duplicates.
Body: { status: 'applied' | 'saved' | 'ignored' }. Cannot set 'missed' (system only — returns 403).
Returns { saved: N, applied: N, missed: N } for dashboard summary strip.
?status=saved|applied|missed|all. Full activity list with listing data populated.
?page&limit=20. Sorted createdAt desc. Both read and unread.
Returns { count: N } for nav badge.
Sets status: 'read'. userId check prevents cross-user modification.
Sets all unread notifications for the user to read.
Create listing manually. Auto-tag runs. AuditLog: 'created_manually'.
Full update. Optimistic concurrency on version. Triggers notifications on cancel/dont-miss.
Atomic: lastDeadline←deadline, clear dates, status←upcoming, isCurated←false, version++.
Sets lastVerifiedAt: now, isStale: false. AuditLog entry.
Sets status: closed, records rejection reason in AuditLog.
5.7 Admin — Sources, Tags, Guides, Users API
All active DomainTags grouped by category (student-facing).
Create new tag. Slug auto-generated from displayName.
Guide only if isPublished: true. 404 otherwise.
Save/publish guide. Sets Listing.guideId on linked listings.
All cron jobs are implemented using node-cron and run within the Express process. Event-driven dispatches are triggered inline by the relevant API handler.
Find listings where status='upcoming' AND openDate ≤ now. Batch update status='open'. AuditLog action: 'auto_opened' for each.
Find listings where status='open' AND deadline ≤ now. For each: if lastDeadline is null, copy deadline → lastDeadline. Set status='closed'. AuditLog action: 'auto_closed'.
Find UserActivity records where status='saved' AND the associated listing's deadline ≤ now. Batch update status='missed', statusUpdatedAt = listing.deadline (not current time). AuditLog.

3-day reminder: find UserActivity where status='saved' AND listing.deadline = today+3 days. For each user: check notificationPrefs.deadlineReminders = true AND no existing deadline_3day notification for this (userId, listingId) pair. Create Notification. If emailEnabled: send email.
1-day reminder: same logic for listing.deadline = today+1 day. type: deadline_1day. In-app notification only (no email).
Frequency cap enforcement: before creating any dont_miss notification, query Notifications where type='dont_miss' AND userId=X AND createdAt > 7 days ago. Skip if found.
6.3 Staleness Flag Cron — Every Sunday at 02:00
Find listings where status in [upcoming, open] AND (lastVerifiedAt < 270 days ago OR lastVerifiedAt is null). Set isStale: true. AuditLog: 'auto_flagged_stale'.
Find listings where timeline.lastDeadline is older than 2 years. Set isStale: true if not already set.
6.4 Season Window Cron — 1st of every month at 09:00
Find listings where timeline.expectedStart = currentMonth AND status = 'upcoming'.
For each listing: find users with this listing's domainTags in their interests AND notificationPrefs.seasonAlerts = true AND no season_open notification for this listing received this year.
Cap: 1 per domain category per user per month. If user already has season_open for another listing in same category this month, skip.
Create Notification docs (type: season_open, in-app only).
Triggered immediately when admin sets priority = 'dont-miss' via PUT /api/admin/listings/:id. Not a cron job.
Find all users where interests array intersects with listing's domainTags.
Apply frequency cap: skip users who received a dont_miss notification in the past 7 days.
Create Notification docs (type: dont_miss). In-app only.
Triggered immediately when admin sets status = 'cancelled'. Not a cron job.
Query UserActivity for all users with saved or applied status for the cancelled listing.
Create Notification docs (type: cancelled) for all affected users.
In-app notification + email if user.notificationPrefs.emailEnabled = true.
Runs as a background worker separate from the cron jobs (or as a frequently-scheduled cron). Ensures the Recommended feed section can be served from Redis without on-demand computation.
For each active user (status: active, onboardingComplete: true): run the eligibility filter + scoring query (Section FR-FEED-04 and FR-FEED-05 in the SRS).
Store top 8 results as JSON in Redis: key = rec:{userId}, TTL = 30 minutes.
On feed load, GET /api/feed/sections reads rec:{userId} from Redis. On cache miss, compute on demand and populate cache.
7.1 Student Discovers and Saves a Listing
Student opens /app/feed. React Query fires three parallel calls: GET /api/feed/sections (reads recommended from Redis cache), GET /api/activity/summary, GET /api/notifications/unread-count.
Feed renders. Student sees 'Google STEP 2026' in the Recommended for You section with a score derived from matching domainTags.
Student taps the Save button. The UI immediately updates the card to 'Saved' state (optimistic update). POST /api/activity fires in the background.

Backend: verify JWT. Check UserActivity unique constraint — no existing record for this (userId, listingId). Create UserActivity { userId, listingId, status: 'saved', statusUpdatedAt: now, createdAt: now }. Response 201.
If the API call fails (network error, duplicate, etc.): the optimistic update rolls back. Card reverts to unsaved state. A toast error notification is shown.

That evening, the notification cron (08:00) checks if listing.deadline = today+3. If yes and user.notificationPrefs.deadlineReminders = true: create Notification { type: deadline_3day, payload snapshot, expiresAt: deadline }. Student sees badge on next visit.
Admin opens /admin/listings/new. Empty curation panel renders.
Admin fills all fields: title, description, type, timeline, targetAudience (years=[2,3], branches=[CSE,IT]), domainTags, status=open, priority=high, isCurated=true.

Admin clicks Save. POST /api/admin/listings. Backend: validate JWT + role:admin. Run auto-tagger (keyword suggestions from description — admin-set tags take precedence). Insert listing document with version=1.
AuditLog written: { category: 'listing', action: 'created_manually', actorType: 'user', actorId: adminUserId, diff: null }.

Listing is now live (status: open). Year-2 and year-3 CSE/IT students with frontend or matching interests will see it in Recommended on their next feed load (after the next recommendation pre-compute cycle).
Status transition cron runs hourly. Detects listing.deadline ≤ now. Sets status='closed'. Copies deadline → lastDeadline (if null). Writes AuditLog: 'auto_closed'.
Simultaneously: finds all UserActivity records for this listing with status='saved'. Batch updates status='missed', statusUpdatedAt = listing.deadline. Writes AuditLog per record.
Listing disappears from student feed (feed query filters to status in [open, upcoming]). Students' tracking dashboards now show it under the Missed section.
Admin reviews the closed listing. Next year's dates are available. Admin opens the curation panel and clicks Cycle Reset. A confirmation dialog explains the action.

POST /api/admin/listings/:id/cycle-reset: atomic update — lastDeadline preserved (previous year's deadline), openDate and deadline cleared, status='upcoming', isCurated=false, version++. AuditLog: 'cycle_reset' with full diff.
Listing re-enters upcoming state. Admin fills in new year's dates and sets isCurated=true. Listing becomes active for the next season.
Organisation logs in to /org with their pre-registered credentials (role: source). Opens the Organisation Listing Manager.
Organisation fills in the new listing form and submits. POST /api/org/listings. Backend: create Listing with status='unknown', isCurated=false, sourceId pointing to the Organisation's Source record.
AuditLog: { category: 'listing', action: 'submitted_by_org', actorType: 'source' }. Listing enters the admin queue.
Admin sees +1 badge on the Submission Queue nav item. Opens /admin/queue. The new submission appears with status: unknown.

Admin clicks Approve. Navigates to full curation panel pre-filled with the submitted data. Admin fills missing fields (domainTags, targetAudience, timeline), sets status=open, isCurated=true, and saves.
PUT /api/admin/listings/:id. Listing goes live. AuditLog records the full edit with diff showing the unknown→open transition and all filled-in fields.
Client-side routing with URL-persisted filter state.
Server state management, caching, optimistic updates.
Markdown rendering for listing descriptions and guides.
MongoDB ODM. Schema validation, query building.
Request body validation. Returns 422 with field-level errors.
In-process cron scheduler for background jobs.
SMTP email dispatch for verification and notifications.
Primary data store. Free tier (512 MB).
React SPA CDN hosting. Automatic CI/CD.
Express API hosting. ~30 s cold-start accepted.
The following V2 features require no breaking changes to V1 schemas or API contracts. All additions are strictly additive.
Source.sourceType includes scraper. Ingestion path is identical.
Scraper service, deduplication logic, per-source admin review toggle.
DomainTag.roadmapUrl field exists. Slugs match roadmap.sh path conventions.
Display link in student UI. Optional: seed tags from roadmap.sh API.
orgName and orgLogoUrl exist as flat fields on Listing.
Extract to Organisation collection. Add orgId FK. Migrate existing values.
User model has no conflicting OAuth fields.
Add googleId field. Passport.js Google strategy. Skip email verification for OAuth.
UserActivity has createdAt + statusUpdatedAt. Listing has full timeline.
Frontend calendar component filtered to user's saved/applied activity.
MongoDB Atlas Search index. GET /api/feed/search?q= endpoint.
UserActivity has all needed data for collaborative filtering.
Aggregation: users with same branch+year+interests who saved this listing.
Aggregation: median of (statusUpdatedAt - createdAt) where status=applied.
UserAsset collection. CRUD endpoints. Link to activity on apply.
confidenceLevel and dataSourceYear are never stored in MongoDB. They are computed at read time by the API layer and attached to the response object before it is returned to the client.
confidenceLevel = 'confirmed'; // Active deadline set for this cycle
confidenceLevel = 'estimated'; // Based on last cycle (within 1 year)
End of High-Level Design Document — COA-HLD-001 v1.0
